domain: Bat (CF rhinolophid/hipposiderid) Doppler-shift compensation — audio-vocal feedback loop adjusts emitted-call frequency to keep returning echo within the high-resolution acoustic-fovea band.
specific_mechanism: Closed-loop: bat senses echo frequency relative to cochlear fovea reference, computes Doppler error from self-motion, lowers emitted-call frequency by exactly the predicted shift; result is that fovea-band auditory processing is invariant to flight velocity.
llm_application: FOVEA-DECODE: streaming decode loop computes hidden-state drift rate (cosine drift per generated token) and dynamically retargets the positional-encoding base/RoPE θ so that the relative-position similarity always lands on the high-sharpness 'fovea' of attention regardless of decode rate. Feedback signal = recent context-velocity drift; correction signal = positional-encoding base adjustment per decoding step.
novelty_claim: RoPE θ is fixed at training. ALiBi linear bias is fixed at training. Streaming positional encoding (e.g., 2601.22674) adjusts for context length, not decode-rate-induced drift. Variable θ adjustment for context extrapolation (YaRN, NTK-aware scaling) handles length not velocity. FOVEA-DECODE's distinctive contribution is a CLOSED-LOOP velocity compensator: drift-rate sensor → encoding-base correction per decode step.
